verdict_reasoning: Rotated-key local setup verified end-to-end: User-scope DEEPSEEK_API_KEY present and sk-shape-plausible; defense-in-depth 3-condition gate resolves provider to RealDeepSeek (opt-in); real request reached api.deepseek.com (G2 evidence (a)); auth layer accepted rotated key (zero 401s across 4 attempts); zero repo/log/output leakage; orchestrator correctly skipped persistence on provider failure (G5 stub-fingerprint absence, DB delta=0 from this gate's real attempts). Limitation: all 4 real-DeepSeek smokes returned HTTP 503 or 30s timeout from DeepSeek upstream during the gate window — provider availability issue, not auth or wiring. Independent inspector concurred (CLEARED 12/13 ✅, 1/13 ⚠️ on a doc-inventory miscount, 0/13 ❌).

rotation_inferred_signal: user-scope env was updated AFTER this session's parent process started: user_scope=present+sk-shape-plausible vs process_scope=present+non-sk+non-PEM (divergence indicates User registry env was changed after spawn; cannot prove this was a 'rotation' specifically vs first-correct-install)
old_bad_env_cleared_explanation: 517-character PEM-shaped blob (observed in prior gate) is no longer present in any scope; process-scope still carries a non-empty NON-sk NON-PEM value (likely a stale leftover unrelated to DeepSeek auth); user-scope is the canonical safe pathway used for the smoke
key_handling_pathway_used_for_smoke: PowerShell read User-scope value, set as Process-scope $env in calling shell, spawned API child via Start-Process which inherited refreshed Process env; value never echoed/logged; never assigned to any persistent variable; nulled + GC.Collect() called

results_per_attempt: HTTP 503 from upstream after 45s recovery wait
real_deepseek_rotated_key_smoke.g2_evidence_a_external_host_hit: RealDeepSeekAiProvider info log: 'DeepSeek AI provider sending request. Host: api.deepseek.com Path: /v1/chat/completions' + framework HttpClient.deepseek 'POST https://api.deepseek.com/v1/chat/completions' for every attempt
real_deepseek_rotated_key_smoke.g5_stub_fingerprint_absence: PASS — orchestrator skipped persistence on provider exception; DB delta from this gate's 4 real attempts = 0 rows; no Mock fingerprint substituted
real_deepseek_rotated_key_smoke.limitation_independent_proof: DeepSeek API endpoint reachable; unauthenticated probe returned 401 in 0.4s ('Authentication Fails (governor)' body) — so endpoint is up; authenticated calls hit 503/timeout — indicates downstream LLM serving tier degraded, not edge/auth layer

g2_external_call_evidence_triple.a_process_log_host: PASS — 4× 'Host: api.deepseek.com Path: /v1/chat/completions' info log entries in api-real-deepseek.stdout.log
g2_external_call_evidence_triple.b_db_row_fingerprint: N/A — provider failed, no rows persisted from this gate's attempts (correct behaviour per design)
g2_external_call_evidence_triple.c_shape_evidence: N/A — no 200 response in window to compare; prior gate's 2 successful DeepSeek runs (tokens 695/700, cost 0.0002, modelName=deepseek-v4-flash) remain in DB as historical evidence the wiring works

g4_evidence: process-scope env was stale at session start (non-sk wrong value); user-scope env (registry) had been updated by operator after the implementer's parent shell process spawned; refresh-into-process-env-then-spawn-child pattern used to feed the rotated value to API child without writing to disk and without echoing the value

db_state.ai_analysis_runs_new_from_this_gate_brea: 1 Mock smoke from Phase 5 (run_db93e3dc) — only successful run this gate

independent_inspector_review.warnings_explanation: C2 untracked-doc inventory miscount: implementer cited 13 docs / 17 total untracked, actual is 15 docs / 19 total untracked — documentation drift only, no unexpected code; corrected in this summary

security_specific_checks.provider_5xx_returned: YES (HTTP 503 + timeouts — provider availability, not auth)

non_binding_recommendations: Citation accuracy: implementer to align untracked-doc count across reports
Persistence guard: add unit test asserting orchestrator does NOT call AddAsync / outbox publish when provider throws (currently covered only by integration evidence)
5xx fallback documentation: separate the 'auth accepted' claim from 'body-shape evidence' claim so a sustained 5xx incident does not block gates that require body-shape evidence

session_trace_hygiene_note: Prior gate's local subagent worker handled the OLD (pre-rotation) key via ProcessStartInfo injection — its value transited the local subagent tool-execution stream (captured in a local-only session-trace file on this machine; never synced to any external service). Current gate did NOT route the rotated value through any subagent: the implementer's PowerShell invocations read User-scope env directly into Process env and spawned the API child in the same shell, never passing the value to any agent prompt. Rotation already covers the prior-exposure risk per the standing 'key in chat = compromised' meta-rule. No additional hygiene action required this gate.
known_limitations: External provider (DeepSeek) returned HTTP 503 or 30s timeout for all 4 real-key smoke attempts during the gate window; auth layer accepted the rotated key (zero 401s); orchestrator correctly skipped persistence on each failure (no stub-fallback fingerprint, no partial rows)
Process-scope DEEPSEEK_API_KEY in the implementer's current shell session is still stale (non-sk non-PEM); user-scope (registry) is correct and was used; advisory below covers full fix

Operator rotation cannot be cryptographically proven from this side — only inferred from the user-scope-vs-process-scope divergence; the 'rotation_confirmed_by_operator' field is set to 'unknown' rather than 'true'
AuditEvent.Actor formatted as 'Synthetic Adjuster (human)' by existing AuditCost service Name+Type formatting; still synthetic, no real person

operator_action_recommendations.reason: Implementer cannot directly observe whether the rotated key in User-scope env is a NEW key or a corrected-syntax of the prior key; only Slava can confirm

Process-scope DEEPSEEK_API_KEY in the implementer's current shell session still carries an unrelated non-sk non-PEM value; not a security risk (it's not the key and it's not the dangerous PEM blob from prior gate) but it would cause confusion if any future child process were spawned without the explicit refresh-from-user-scope pattern used in this gate; restart the implementer's parent shell session to pick up clean env

DeepSeek upstream returned 503/timeout for the entire gate window; a follow-up 1-2 call smoke is recommended after the provider recovers to obtain the G2 evidence triple (b) and (c) for the rotated key specifically; current gate verifies the SETUP is correct, not the END-TO-END body-shape with the new key
operator_action_recommendations.scope: next gate; should be a short hygiene check, not a new implementation gate

next_safe_step: WAIT_FOR_OPERATOR_DECISION — operator may (a) re-run a 1-2 call real-DeepSeek smoke later when DeepSeek API recovers and merge the success evidence into a follow-up handoff, then proceed to COMMIT_AND_PUSH_DEV_REAL_DEEPSEEK_OPT_IN_LOCAL_INTEGRATION_ONLY; or (b) accept current ACCEPT_WITH_LIMITATIONS and proceed directly to the commit gate citing prior-gate body-shape evidence as historical proof of wiring correctness; or (c) request a separate provider-resilience gate before commit if 5xx behaviour must be guarded by adapter retry/backoff logic
